meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve Project Stall (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on SaaS builds. Master pricing strategies to ship faster and impress clients using Antigravity and Claude Code. Your blueprint for success.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is often 'scope creep' masked as 'feature perfection.' By implementing tiered SaaS pricing, you force yourself to define clear MVP boundaries. Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to execute the logic, ensuring you ship a profitable, limited-scope product on time.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a half-finished codebase, the momentum long dead, while your client's deadline looms like a storm cloud. It’s not a lack of talent—it’s a lack of structure. Most freelancers fail to finish because they treat every project like a sprawling monolith, drowning in features that nobody is paying for.

True mastery isn't just about writing code; it's about engineering constraints. By aligning your technical architecture with a tiered pricing model, you transform your project from a bottomless pit into a high-velocity sprint. Let's strip away the fluff and build exactly what your client needs, using modern AI tooling to bridge the gap between idea and deployment.

steps.title: Define the MVP via Tiered Pricing
steps.description: Don't build features until they are priced. Create three tiers: Basic (Core problem), Pro (Workflow efficiency), and Enterprise (Security/Compliance). This forces you to identify the 'must-haves'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a feature breakdown for a SaaS product based on a 3-tier pricing model: Basic, Pro, and Enterprise. Focus on minimal viable features for the Basic tier that solve the core problem.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a JSON schema for a SaaS pricing model based on the core problem: [Insert Problem]'

steps.title: Architect for Incremental Deployment
steps.description: Structure your code so that the Basic tier is a standalone module. This prevents you from getting stuck on 'nice-to-have' features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Architect a modular directory structure for a SaaS app where the core functionality is decoupled from premium features, allowing for independent deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Scaffold a project structure that separates core auth and database logic from premium feature modules.'

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate with Antigravity
steps.description: Stop writing auth and database schemas from scratch. Use AI to generate the heavy lifting so you can focus on the unique value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure boilerplate for a SaaS app using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe integration, optimized for speed.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the authentication and billing subscription flow using the current project structure.'

steps.title: Implement Pricing Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Integrate your pricing tiers directly into the codebase. This ensures the UI/UX naturally guides the user toward the Pro tier.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the logic to gate features based on user subscription status retrieved from the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement a feature flagging system that checks user subscription status before rendering premium components.'

steps.title: Ship and Iterate
steps.description: Deploy to staging immediately. The goal is to get a functional URL to the client, not a perfect codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment script that pushes the current codebase to Vercel and runs a sanity check on the pricing flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Verify the deployment pipeline is configured correctly and run a test build.'

pro_tips: Always price the 'Basic' tier to cover your development costs, not just your time.
Use feature flags to hide incomplete features instead of commenting out code.
If a feature takes more than 4 hours to build, it belongs in a future 'Pro' update, not the MVP.
Charge your clients for the 'architecture' phase, not just the code. It builds authority.
Use Claude Code to write tests for your billing logic first; if the money doesn't work, the code doesn't matter.
content.next_steps: Once the MVP is deployed, use user feedback to determine which 'Pro' feature to build next. Never guess what the user wants—let their subscription data tell you.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop stalling and start shipping. Don't let another project languish in the 'almost done' folder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
